Contact

MP for Aberdeenshire North and Moray East

Welome to the official website of Seamus Logan MP

"As your Member of Parliament, my priority is to represent the needs and concerns of our community at every level. Whether it’s standing up for local services, supporting families and businesses, or ensuring your voice is heard in Westminster, I am committed to working tirelessly on your behalf. Every decision I make is guided by one simple principle: putting people first."

Staying Informed

Welcome to the news and updates section. Here you’ll find the latest statements, press releases, and updates from Seamus Logan MP — keeping constituents informed about local issues, parliamentary work, and community initiatives.

Featured News Article

Scottish Hauliers Hit Breaking Point due to Westminster ‘Fuel Tax Hike’

The Westminster government’s shambolic handling of fuel prices is pushing haulage firms in North-east Scotland to the brink by volatile costs and a lack of support.

The Westminster government is preparing to reintroduce fuel duty in stages from September, which I believe will act like...

  • 22 April 2026

    Challenging Assumptions on Multiple Sclerosis for MS Awareness Week 2026

    I’m supporting the MS Society’s campaign to challenge assumptions on Multiple Sclerosis during MS Awareness Week…

Contact SeamusAll News & updates

Before becoming an MP, I was a mental health social worker, working at all levels before retiring in 2013 as regional director. For much of the next decade, I worked as a coach driver and tourist guide across the region.

My interest in politics goes back to my student days, but later I was active as a campaigner for a number of politicians in Ireland.

I then joined the SNP in 2018, shortly after moving to Fraserburgh, and went on to campaign in local and national elections. This included the 2022 election to Aberdeenshire Council for the Fraserburgh and District Ward.

About Seamus

I was born in Ballymoney in the 1950s in County Antrim, Northern Ireland, and brought up in Dunloy, a good grounding for living now in the Broch as we call Fraserburgh. I feel a great affinity with the people and communities who live here and I appreciate the welcome I received from them when I first arrived. The Northern Irish have a lot in common with folk from this corner of Scotland – forthright, a wee bit brusque, hard-working, industrious, and imaginative. It’s the people that make it here and I am glad to be one of them now.

It’s the people too that are at the heart of everything I do as their MP – I want to put them first, to stand up for them and act as a voice for their concerns at Westminster, no matter party affiliation or none. And I’m well aware of what they have entrusted to me to do as their elected representative.

Working hard for the people, every day.

From tackling local issues on the ground to shaping national policy, I believe in being a visible and approachable MP. That means listening to constituents, holding regular surgeries, and working alongside community groups, charities, and businesses to deliver real results. Together, we can build a stronger, fairer future for everyone in our constituency.

Questions Asked

As your Member of Parliament, my priority is to represent the needs and concerns of our community at every level. Whether it’s standing up for local services, supporting families and businesses, or ensuring your voice is heard in Westminster, I am committed to working tirelessly on your behalf. Every decision I make is guided by one simple principle: putting people first.

Constituancy & Parliamentary Offices

You can write to Seamus at any of his two main Offices. They are located as follows. If you would like to meet with Seamus, please visit the upcoming surgeries page using the button below.

Parliamentary office

Address:
House of Commons,
London,
SW1A 0AA
Email:
seamus.logan.mp@parliament.uk

Constituency office

Address:
Unit 21,
Fraserburgh Business Centre,
South Harbour Road,
Fraserburgh,
Aberdeenshire,
AB43 9TN
Email:
seamus.logan.mp@parliament.ukPhone:
01346 487013

This website is funded from the Parliamentary allowances provided by the taxpayer. It has been prepared to assist Seamus Logan MP in their parliamentary duties. It is not funded for party political purposes.
